items_en: 1. Takes note of the report of the Secretary-General; 4

2. Recognizes that some countries have been successful in adapting to change and have benefited from globalization, but that many others, in particular the least developed countries, remain on the margins of a globalized economy and that, as emphasized in the Millennium Declaration, the benefits of globalization are very unevenly distributed and the burdens they impose unevenly;

3. Also recognizes that national economies are now intertwined in the global economy, that globalization affects each country differently and that, on the one hand, globalization offers opportunities for trade and investment, including to combat poverty, and, on the other, it imposes constraints on the implementation of national development strategies;

4. Further recognizes that, while all countries face such constraints, developing countries, in particular the least developed countries, landlocked developing countries, small island developing States and African countries, have greater difficulty in achieving a balance between international obligations, procedures and rules and their national development strategies;

5. Stresses that, in considering the linkages between globalization and sustainable development, efforts should be made to establish and implement mutually reinforcing policies and practices that promote sustained economic growth, social development and environmental protection, and that this requires efforts at the national and international levels;

6. Also stresses that the growing interdependence of national economies in a globalizing world and the establishment of systems regulating international economic relations mean that the policy space of countries in the economic field, namely, the scope of national policies, particularly in the areas of trade, investment and industrial development, is today often bounded by international rules and commitments and by global market considerations, that it is for each Government to balance the benefits of accepting international rules and commitments with the constraints that will limit its policy space, and that it is particularly important for developing countries, taking into account development objectives, that all countries recognize the need to reconcile national policy space with international rules and commitments, and, in this regard, notes with appreciation the work of the United Nations Conference on Trade and Development on the concept of policy space, as expressed, inter alia, in the SÃĢo Paulo Consensus 5 and the 2005 World Summit Outcome 1;
7. Reaffirms that good governance is the prerequisite for sustainable development, as well as sound economic policies and strong institutions

text_en: democracy that meets the needs of the population and improved infrastructure is the basis for sustainable economic growth, poverty eradication and job creation, and that freedom, peace and security, internal stability, respect for human rights, including the right to development, and the rule of law, respect for gender equality, market-oriented policies and a comprehensive commitment to a just and democratic society are also essential elements that are mutually reinforcing;

9. RÃĐaffirme en outre que chaque pays est responsable au premier chef de son propre dÃĐveloppement, que l'on ne saurait exagÃĐrer le rÃīle des politiques et des stratÃĐgies  nationales  de  dÃĐveloppement  dans  la  rÃĐalisation  du  dÃĐveloppement durable et que les efforts fournis par les pays devraient ÃŠtre complÃĐtÃĐs par des politiques, mesures et programmes d'appui mondiaux tendant Ã  offrir aux pays en dÃĐveloppement  de  meilleures  chances  de  dÃĐveloppement,  compte  tenu  de  leur situation  et  dans  le  respect  de  leurs  prÃĐrogatives,  stratÃĐgies  et  souverainetÃĐ nationales ;

Souligne  ÃĐgalement que  tous  les  pays  en  dÃĐveloppement  doivent conserver leur droit de poursuivre des politiques adaptÃĐes Ã  leurs caractÃĐristiques sociales, politiques, ÃĐconomiques et environnementales et que, Ã  cet ÃĐgard, les pays, en particulier les pays en dÃĐveloppement, ont besoin de disposer de souplesse et d'une  marge  d'action  nationale  compatible  avec  leurs  conditions  particuliÃĻres, compte tenu des obligations, procÃĐdures et rÃĻgles internationales ;
12. Estime qu'il est nÃĐcessaire que les institutions commerciales multilatÃĐrales continuent d'adopter des dispositions spÃĐciales et souples en faveur des pays en dÃĐveloppement ;

RÃĐaffirme la volontÃĐ d'associer plus largement les pays en dÃĐveloppement et les pays en transition Ã  la prise des dÃĐcisions et Ã  l'ÃĐtablissement de normes internationales dans le domaine ÃĐconomique, souligne qu'Ã  cette fin il importe de continuer Ã  s'efforcer de rÃĐformer l'architecture financiÃĻre
items_en: 8. Also reaffirms that good governance at the international level is a fundamental condition for achieving sustainable development, and that, in order to create a dynamic and enabling international economic environment, it is important to promote global economic governance by addressing international financial, trade, technology and investment mechanisms that have an impact on the development prospects of developing countries and that, to this end, the international community should take all appropriate measures, including ensuring support for structural and macroeconomic reforms, providing a comprehensive solution to the external debt problem and expanding market access for developing countries;

9. Further reaffirms that each country is primarily responsible for its own development, that the role of national development policies and strategies cannot be overstated in achieving sustainable development, and that national efforts should be complemented by global policies, measures and programmes of support aimed at improving the development opportunities of developing countries, taking into account their circumstances and respecting their national prerogatives, strategies and sovereignty;

10. Stresses that, while recognizing the commitment of developing countries to respect their international obligations, lessons learned and new realities must be taken into account in the application of internationally agreed rules and disciplines, taking into account the special circumstances of developing countries, and in this regard reaffirms the need for the continued adoption of special and flexible arrangements by the international financial and trade institutions for developing countries in the formulation and implementation of rules and regulations aimed at creating an international environment conducive to widespread and sustained growth;

11. Also stresses that all developing countries should maintain their right to pursue policies that are appropriate to their social, political, economic and environmental characteristics and that, in this regard, countries, in particular developing countries, need flexibility and national policy space consistent with their specific conditions, taking into account international obligations, procedures and rules;
12. Recognizes the need for multilateral trading institutions to continue to adopt special and flexible provisions for developing countries;

13. Reaffirms the desire to increase the participation of developing countries and countries with economies in transition in international economic decision-making and norm-setting, stresses the importance of continuing efforts to reform the financial architecture to this end

text_fr: internationale,  en  notant  que  donner  davantage  voix  au  chapitre  aux  pays  en dÃĐveloppement et aux pays en transition et leur assurer une participation accrue au sein des institutions de Bretton Woods demeurent une prÃĐoccupation constante, et demande que soient accomplis de nouveaux progrÃĻs tangibles Ã  cet ÃĐgard ;

15. Demande aux  organismes  des  Nations  Unies  d'aider  les  pays  en dÃĐveloppement  pour  leur  permettre  de  mieux  ÃĐvaluer  l'impact  des  accords internationaux sur leur propre stratÃĐgie de dÃĐveloppement ;

16. Prie le SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral de lui prÃĐsenter Ã  sa soixante-troisiÃĻme session un rapport sur le thÃĻme ÂŦ Impact de la mondialisation sur la rÃĐalisation des objectifs de  dÃĐveloppement  convenus  au  niveau  international,  y  compris  les  objectifs  du MillÃĐnaire pour le dÃĐveloppement Âŧ, au titre de la question intitulÃĐe ÂŦ Mondialisation et interdÃĐpendance Âŧ ;

items_en: 14. Also reaffirms the need for the United Nations to play a decisive role in promoting international cooperation for development and in ensuring coherence, coordination and implementation of the goals and measures agreed upon by the international community, and undertakes to strengthen coordination within the United Nations system, in close cooperation with other multilateral financial, trade and development institutions, in order to promote sustained economic growth, poverty eradication and sustainable development;
15. Calls upon the United Nations system to assist developing countries in better assessing the impact of international agreements on their own development strategies;

16. Requests the Secretary-General to submit to the General Assembly at its sixty-third session a report on the theme "Impact of globalization on the achievement of the internationally agreed development goals, including the Millennium Development Goals", under the item entitled "Globalization and interdependence";

17. Decides to include in the provisional agenda of its sixty-third session, under the item entitled "Globalization and interdependence", a sub-item entitled "The role of the United Nations in promoting development in the context of globalization and interdependence".

text_en: 78th plenary meeting 19 December 2007
